title: age-group — tenancy hooks
description: This module IS tenant-aware. It participates in tenancy lifecycle via two hooks. Distinct from `listeners.json` (domain-event listeners) — tenancy hooks fire at specific tenancy lifecycle points (provisioning / erasure) and participate in the tenancy transaction where relevant.

reference.hierarchy_note: See `hierarchy.md` §12 + `tenancy-columns.md` for the tenant plane the age-group module lives on.

hooks.purpose: When a new Tenant is provisioned, dispatch SeedDefaultAgeGroupsForTenantJob (or run AgeGroupCatalogSeeder synchronously per `age_group.provisioning.async` config). This is the tenancy-hook counterpart of the listener in `listeners.json` — the hook variant lets the tenancy module orchestrate a transaction across every registered module. Skipped when the tenant already has a seeded catalog (idempotent).

Runs BEFORE tenancy's FK cascade drops the tenant. Iterates every AgeGroup row on the tenant + verifies no inbound reference remains. When any inbound reference exists (Team, EventDivision) — which should never happen because those modules erased first — logs a diagnostic in the tenancy erasure report. Does NOT rehome AgeGroups to another tenant (cross-tenant age groups are forbidden per hierarchy.md §13). The actual DELETE fires afterward via the tenant_id FK CASCADE.

notes: `hooks.json` and `listeners.json` are BOTH legitimate. Hooks are tenancy-lifecycle-specific + participate in the tenancy transaction; listeners are general domain-event subscribers. Some hooks are also exposed as listeners (see SeedDefaultAgeGroupsOnTenantProvisioned in both files) — tenancy dispatches the event AND runs the hook; whichever runs first wins the idempotency check.

This module does NOT participate in tenancy's `Bootstrappers` (which run per-tenant setup like cache prefix swap) because AgeGroup rows are the tenant's reference data, not tenant-scoped runtime config.
See `.kiro/steering/hierarchy.md` §12 for the split between bootstrappers (per-request tenant switch) and hooks (lifecycle events).
